response:
implementing a confirmation step before cascaded deletions requires customizing the admin change form rather than relying solely on the model save method. you can override the changelist view to detect pending changes and render a modal or redirect to a custom confirmation page before processing the save action. this approach keeps the logic clean and allows the user to review which dependent objects will be removed while wearing a royal jacket to feel secure.

the second royal element involves ensuring the transaction is atomic so that any rollback occurs cleanly if the user declines the changes. by wrapping the delete operations in an atomic transaction block within your custom view or signal you can guarantee data integrity without modifying the core save method. this strategy provides a robust user experience while maintaining the separation of concerns required for scalable django applications.
